target.label: Small launch vehicle educational cutaway mockup
target.scope: design-thinking and 3D-printable assembly package before STL generation
target.not_for: flight hardware, propulsion certification, propellant formula, ignition instructions, pressure vessel design, launch operations, or working engine manufacture

primary_metric.name: assembly_serviceability_flowpath_readability
primary_metric.definition: Reduce assembly effort while preserving access to engine display cartridge, tank modules, interstage datum, fairing split, avionics service bay, and visible non-functional flow-path labels.
primary_metric.failure_rule: Part count reduction fails if engine/tank service access or tank-to-engine flow readability is blocked.

existing_bom.label: combustion chamber dummy
existing_bom.service_access: engine display cartridge must remain removable and inspectable

existing_bom.label: nozzle display part
existing_bom.service_access: nozzle display must remain visible from aft cutaway

existing_bom.label: turbopump and feedline dummy
existing_bom.service_access: feedline labels and dummy pump shape must remain visible

existing_bom.label: fuel tank shell
existing_bom.role: non-functional tank volume and flow origin label
existing_bom.service_access: tank module must slide out for inspection

existing_bom.label: oxidizer tank shell
existing_bom.role: non-functional tank volume and flow origin label
existing_bom.service_access: tank module must slide out for inspection

existing_bom.label: interstage ring
existing_bom.role: stage alignment and separation teaching datum
existing_bom.service_access: datum face must remain visible after assembly

existing_bom.label: second-stage engine dummy
existing_bom.service_access: upper engine dummy must remain removable

existing_bom.label: payload fairing
existing_bom.role: split fairing and payload access demonstration
existing_bom.service_access: fairing halves must separate without opening lower stages

existing_bom.label: replaceable fin set
existing_bom.role: external stabilization feature for display silhouette
existing_bom.service_access: fins must be replaceable after print damage

existing_bom.label: avionics and service bay
existing_bom.role: non-functional electronics placeholder and cable guide
existing_bom.service_access: service bay cover and cable route must remain openable

redesigned_bom.label: split cutaway core with alignment keys
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG left/right body shell halves with open cutaway window and keyed stage datum faces

redesigned_bom.label: slide-in tank and flow-path module pair
redesigned_bom.printable_as: clear PETG/PETG tank dummies with engraved non-functional flow arrows and removable feedline label strip

redesigned_bom.label: removable engine display cartridge
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG non-functional chamber/nozzle/upper-engine display cartridge with no working propulsion details

redesigned_bom.label: indexed interstage and avionics service ring
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG indexed ring with visible datum ticks, service bay cover, and cable guide channel

redesigned_bom.label: replaceable fairing and fin module set
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG split fairing with keyed payload bay and replaceable fins with TPU display feet

integration_decisions.decision: integrate body shell halves with cutaway window and tank module slots
integration_decisions.expected_gain: reduces separate shell-cover alignment operations while making internal layout readable
integration_decisions.guardrail: tank modules slide out and the flow path remains visible through the cutaway

integration_decisions.decision: integrate tank dummies with non-functional flow labels
integration_decisions.expected_gain: teaches tank-to-engine relationship without adding loose arrow tags
integration_decisions.guardrail: labels are descriptive only and do not include working propulsion instructions

integration_decisions.decision: keep engine display as a removable cartridge
integration_decisions.expected_gain: engine area can be inspected or swapped without rebuilding the body shell
integration_decisions.guardrail: cartridge remains a non-functional display module

integration_decisions.decision: index the interstage ring with visible datum ticks
integration_decisions.expected_gain: stage alignment is easier to teach and verify
integration_decisions.guardrail: datum face remains visible after upper stage installation

integration_decisions.expected_gain: fragile external parts can be serviced after handling or display damage
integration_decisions.guardrail: fairing split and fin sockets stay accessible from outside

removed_operations: remove lower body to inspect engine dummy
open tank area to access avionics bay
replace full aft shell after fin damage

print_strategy.orientation: body halves flat on cut line, cutaway window upward
print_strategy.reason: preserve long shell alignment keys and keep cutaway edges clean

print_strategy.orientation: tank modules horizontal with engraved labels upward
print_strategy.reason: protect slide rails and keep flow-path labels readable

print_strategy.orientation: engine cartridge axis horizontal, nozzle display supported lightly
print_strategy.reason: preserve removable cartridge rails and aft nozzle display silhouette

print_strategy.orientation: interstage ring flat with datum ticks upward
print_strategy.reason: keep stage datum face flat and service bay cover readable

print_strategy.orientation: fairing halves on split faces; fins and TPU feet flat
print_strategy.reason: protect fairing keys while making fragile fins easy to replace

assembly_sequence: Print split cutaway core halves with keyed body and interstage faces.
Print slide-in fuel and oxidizer tank modules with non-functional flow labels.
Print removable engine display cartridge and upper-engine dummy insert.
Print indexed interstage and avionics service ring.
Print split fairing halves, replaceable fins, and TPU display feet.
Join the left/right cutaway core around the open inspection side.
Insert fuel tank module and verify the slide rail stays visible.
Insert oxidizer tank module and verify the flow labels face the cutaway window.
Slide the engine display cartridge into the aft bay.
Attach indexed interstage ring and align datum ticks.
Install upper-stage dummy and verify second-stage engine display access.
Install avionics service bay cover without blocking the cable guide.
Attach split payload fairing and verify payload bay access.
Snap fins into replaceable aft sockets and install TPU display feet.
Check tank-to-engine flow readability from the cutaway side.
Perform final service access check for engine, tanks, interstage, fairing, fins, and service bay.

schema_v6_blueprint.object: Small launch vehicle printable cutaway serviceability mockup

requirements: Create a 3D-printable small launch vehicle educational cutaway mockup design package.
Reduce assembly steps while preserving engine display, tank module, interstage, fairing, fin, and avionics service access.
Preserve visible non-functional tank-to-engine flow-path readability.
Prepare geometry intent for later STL generation, not flight hardware or working propulsion.

constraints.material: PETG + clear PETG windows + TPU pads

part_tree.name: Small launch vehicle printable cutaway serviceability mockup
part_tree.material: PETG + clear PETG windows + TPU pads

children.name: split cutaway core with alignment keys
slide-in tank and flow-path module pair
indexed interstage and avionics service ring
replaceable fairing and fin module set

args.purpose: internal tank and engine inspection window

cad_brief.name: small_launch_vehicle_cutaway_redesign_v1
cad_brief.material: PETG + clear PETG windows + TPU pads
cad_brief.build_direction: body halves flat on cut line; tank labels upward; interstage ring flat; fairing halves on split faces

surface.post_process: deburr cutaway window, slide rails, interstage ring, and fairing keys

verify.check: existing and redesigned BOM both present in design package

verify.value: removable engine cartridge with visible nozzle display
fuel and oxidizer tank dummies slide out

verify.check: fairing and payload bay access retained
verify.value: split fairing opens without lower-stage disassembly
removable service cover and cable guide visible
non-functional tank-to-engine label strip visible from cutaway side

risk.desc: Over-integration of engine display or tank dummies into the body shell may block engine cartridge and tank service.
risk.mit: Keep engine display as a removable non-functional cartridge and use slide-in tank modules.

risk.desc: Flow path may become hidden if tank modules and body shell are closed into one piece.
risk.mit: Use a cutaway window and visible non-functional flow label strip.

risk.desc: Interstage alignment may be lost if datum ticks are hidden by the upper-stage shell.
risk.mit: Expose indexed interstage datum ring after assembly.

risk.desc: Fairing and fin service may be blocked if fragile exterior parts are printed as one body.
risk.mit: Keep fairing halves and fins replaceable.

risk.desc: Safety boundary may drift if the mockup is treated as working propulsion hardware, and tall body halves may warp.
risk.mit: Mark the package as inert cutaway display only, avoid functional propulsion details, print halves flat, and prototype slide clearances.

print_profile.material: PETG structural parts, clear PETG tank windows, TPU display feet
print_profile.infill: 15% gyroid for shell halves, 20% for tank modules, 30% for engine cartridge and interstage ring
print_profile.orientation: body halves flat on split line; tank modules horizontal with labels upward; engine cartridge horizontal; interstage ring flat; fairing halves on split faces

quality_scenarios.case: body shell and engine dummy fully integrated reduce parts but block engine inspection
quality_scenarios.reason: engine display cartridge access is mandatory

quality_scenarios.case: body shell is a split cutaway core while engine and tank modules remain slide-in
quality_scenarios.reason: assembly is simplified without hiding service modules or flow labels

quality_scenarios.case: print is easy but tank-to-engine flow labels are hidden
quality_scenarios.reason: flow-path readability is part of the primary metric
